name: One
description: React (and React Native) meta-framework from the Tamagui team, file-based routing across web and native

name.comment: Official name; single Vite plugin React framework for web and native.

description.value: React framework targeting web and native with a single Vite plugin and fully shared code
description.comment: Value proposition from the homepage and GitHub description.

license.comment: Primarily MIT (Nate Wienert); the LICENSE also acknowledges a BSD-3-Clause portion from Expo Router derivative work.

category.comment: Owns the build/server toolchain (Vite plugin + production Hono server); routing is one part.

active-maintenance.comment: Latest stable release v1.21.1 on 2026-07-14; actively released. Checked 2026-07-15.

initial-release.comment: Publicly announced via Show HN on 2024-10-04 by Nate Wienert.

npm-weekly-downloads.comment: Last-week downloads of the 'one' package. Checked 2026-07-15.

routing-paradigm.comment: File-system routing: every file in the app dir becomes a route; render modes set via filename suffixes.

nested-layout-routes.comment: Nested layouts via _layout.tsx; route groups via (parentheses) folders.

dynamic-params.comment: [slug].tsx dynamic segments, read via useParams() or loaders.

optional-catch-all-params.comment: Catch-all/splat via [...rest].tsx which yields an array of segments.

search-params-api.comment: useRouter().setParams() updates typed route query params; useParams reads them.

route-guards-middleware.comment: _middleware.tsx files; nested middlewares run top-to-bottom with a next() chain, used for auth/request interception.

written-in-typescript.comment: Authored in TypeScript; ships fully-typed file-system routes.

type-safe-paths-links.comment: Auto-generated app/routes.d.ts gives typed Link/navigation against known routes.

type-safe-params.comment: Route params typed via generated route types; useParams and setParams take typed param objects.

type-safe-search-params.comment: setParams params are typed but there is no first-class schema validation of search params (unlike TanStack Router).

codegen-required.comment: Route types are generated into app/routes.d.ts during dev (or via 'one generate-routes'), not pure inference.

type-safety-rating.comment: Typed paths/links and typed params via codegen, but search params are not schema-typed/validated — matches the 'typed params or links with gaps' tier.

route-data-loaders.comment: Remix-style 'export loader' data loading that tree-shakes out of client bundles.

actions-mutations.comment: No Remix-style route action primitive; mutations are handled via API routes (route+api.tsx) and middleware.

prefetching.comment: Preloads all JS for initial load and on link hover, without needing a route map.

pending-transition-ui.comment: Built-in loading progress bar; useRouter exposes onLoadState/subscribe for navigation loading state.

per-route-code-splitting.comment: Per-page bundles; server-only loader code tree-shakes out of client bundles.

per-route-error-handling.comment: Custom 404 via +not-found.tsx is documented, but a route-scoped error-boundary primitive for general errors is not clearly documented; could not confirm.

ssr-support.comment: SSR render mode supported (route+ssr.tsx / global ssr mode), but docs note SSR 'just landed' and is less stable than SSG/SPA.

streaming-ssr.comment: Streaming/suspenseful SSR is not explicitly documented; SSR itself is early-stage, so could not confirm.

react-server-components.comment: FAQ explicitly declines RSC: 'we find the complexity trade-off not worth it.'

static-site-generation.comment: SSG is the default render mode; pre-renders pages at build time.

client-only-usable.comment: SPA render mode runs pure client-side rendering with no server.

framework-bound.comment: Requires its own Vite-plugin toolchain and build; not a drop-in router for an arbitrary React app.

bundle-size.comment: Meta-framework — a single 'router bundle' figure isn't meaningful; framework runtime size isn't comparable to a standalone router.

zero-runtime-dependencies.comment: Ships a full framework runtime (Vite plugin, Hono production server, React Native tooling) with many dependencies.

tree-shakeable.comment: Server-only loader code tree-shakes out of client bundles; per-page code splitting.

documentation-quality.comment: Comprehensive docs: routing, render modes, middleware, guides (Tamagui, CRA migration, MDX) and API references, with some newer areas thinner.

official-devtools.comment: Built-in dev tools (Alt+Space): SEO preview, route debugging, loader timing, error tracking, source inspector.

data-fetching-integration.comment: Built-in Remix-style loaders; docs also recommend pairing with a sync engine like Zero for real-time data. No RSC.

predecessor-lineage.value: Started as an experiment porting Expo Router to the vxrn library; built by the Tamagui team
predecessor-lineage.comment: Docs credit the Expo team; internals were then substantially rewritten.
